title: Receipts – whether income or capital – supporting information
description: Information to provide with your objection or private ruling request about receipts.
keywords: income vs capital receipt, income versus capital, capital receipt, capital proceeds, assessable income, deductions, interest,

content_blocks.text: To apply for a private ruling about whether a receipt is of an income or capital nature:

items: complete and submit a private ruling application form
provide the supporting information listed below.

content_blocks.text: To lodge an objection about whether a receipt is of an income or capital nature:

items: complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals
provide the supporting information listed below.

content_blocks.text: Check first whether your question is answered in Assessable income .
Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of the information listed below, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for a private ruling or objection about whether a receipt is of an income or capital nature includes:

items: a full description of the facts leading up to, and surrounding, the receipt
copies of all relevant legal documents – for example, contracts, agreements and settlement deeds.

content_text:
## How to lodge
To apply for a private ruling about whether a receipt is of an income or capital nature:
- complete and submit a private ruling application form
- provide the supporting information listed below.
To lodge an objection about whether a receipt is of an income or capital nature:
- complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals
- provide the supporting information listed below.
Check first whether your question is answered in Assessable income .
## Supporting information
Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of the information listed below, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for a private ruling or objection about whether a receipt is of an income or capital nature includes:
- a full description of the facts leading up to, and surrounding, the receipt
- copies of all relevant legal documents – for example, contracts, agreements and settlement deeds.
